fields.description:
## Why
Sharper detection content in criteria that already exist -- prompt work; findings get sharper, generic passes untouched.

## What (each edits reviewers/plan_review_*.md; all enums enumerated here so the ticket is self-contained)
- E6 verify-command defect taxonomy -- the NINE patterns (E6 currently has 6 checks a-f; add to reach nine): (1) substring false-positive -> require word-boundary/quoted-key anchors; (2) shell-expansion leakage ($VAR/$? inside grep patterns); (3) wrong helper-script argv (cross-check --help); (4) fixture invalidity; (5) missing cardinality assertion when the criterion names a count (jq '.x|length' -eq N); (6) prose-vs-structured assertion target (assert on the artifact with jq -e, not prose); (7) missing prerequisite-state ordering; (8) conflicting ACs (tests-pass vs test-fails-RED) needing a sequencing AC; (9) references sibling-ticket files with no dependency edge. Add corrected-command anchors (grep -E '"cycle_count"[[:space:]]*:' not grep cycle).
- T3 THREE empiricism axes: (a) per-command --help/flag-level empiricism; (b) endpoint granularity (a new endpoint on a used vendor is a new integration); (c) environment preconditions (e.g. HTTP-only env contradicts an OAuth-callback flow). FOUR evidence classes DEFINED: Verified (observed), Partially-verified (integration exists, surface unconfirmed), Unverified (could not probe), Contradicted (environment falsifies it). RECORDED in Pass-1 evidence[] ONLY for Pass-2 to read. CORRECTION (verified): sidecar _slim (sidecar.py:233) DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES evidence[] -- so the four-class is NOT persisted to the slim sidecar; persisting it for calibration is OUT OF SCOPE here (a WS9-style follow-on). No P1Finding schema change (evidence is already list[str]; this is prompt-only).
- G3: three-part coverage standard (same observable outcome / scope matching-or-exceeding / measurable-in-same-terms) + DEFINE three contradiction patterns: bypass-annotation (child annotates/excludes items from the parent metric instead of resolving), scope-narrowing (child covers a narrower condition set), partial-without-remainder (child covers part, does not name the uncovered remainder).
- T5c ambiguity-not-absence access framing; T11 post-rollback SCHEMA-STATE assertion + DATA LOSS RISK; E2 defaultable-gaps coaching; G4 consumer-enumeration precursor (recall->worklist over the existing consumer-impact mode).
Rejected in evaluation: T9 RETIRE-row rule (nit) -- intentionally not a deliverable (kept out of the AC list).

## Provenance
Gap-report labels (G-*/FP-*/R-*/WS-N) are tags from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020), NOT codebase symbols. Symbols introduced by a SIBLING story (e.g. committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim from WS1) are target-state, not pre-existing; the dependency is recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each named rubric file updated; the nine E6 patterns, three T3 axes, four T3 classes, and three G3 patterns are each explicitly present in-prompt.
- [ ] T3 change is PROMPT-ONLY (evidence[] wording): touches no _slim/sidecar code and no P1Finding schema. Proof: `git diff --name-only` lists only reviewers/*.md.
- [ ] CI prompt/routing parity green. Proof: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` exits 0.
- [ ] Affected recall evals updated (incl. R-E6-cutover-no-live-ac). Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py` green.
